headline: Beverage wars: Why Coke's distribution sets it apart from Pepsi
summary: While PepsiCo's (PEP) first quarter earnings results were driven by price cuts across its various food brands — namely its snacks like Doritos — how does the company's beverage segment generally hold up when compared to Coca-Cola (KO)? RBC Capital Markets managing director Nik Modi examines the key differences between Coke and Pepsi's beverage brands and their respective ownership of their distribution process.

headline: New Hopes for Iran Deal — Heard on the Street Thursday Recap
summary: Stocks rose on hopes for Iran talks.  President Trump said Thursday that "we are very close to making a deal" with Iran, and that he was open to traveling to Pakistan to seal an agreement.  The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite indexes both closed at new records again, adding 0.3% and 0.4%, respectively.

headline: PEP Q1 Deep Dive: PepsiCo Delivers Broad-Based Growth as Productivity, Innovation, and Global Events Drive Momentum
summary: Food and beverage company PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 8.5% year on year to $19.44 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.61 per share was 3.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: TSMC's Chip Heat Extends the Record Run, Then Netflix Drops a Double Surprise After the Bell
summary: Thursday was the second consecutive record close for the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq, and for most of the session the driver was straightforward: TSMC's quarterly numbers were the kind of print that leaves the semiconductor bulls feeling vindicated. Then Netflix reported after the bell, and within minutes the stock was down 8% on two pieces of news the market clearly hadn't priced in, a softer forward outlook and the departure of Reed Hastings from the chairman's seat.

raw.headline: TSMC's Chip Heat Extends the Record Run, Then Netflix Drops a Double Surprise After the Bell
